100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 2, 1926, the United States government announced plans to deepen the Panama Canal, aiming to enhance its capacity for maritime traffic. In Europe, the League of Nations faced challenges as debates intensified over disarmament and the ongoing political instability in Germany. Meanwhile, in the realm of science, American inventor Charles Lindbergh's groundbreaking transatlantic flight continued to captivate the public's imagination, solidifying his status as a national hero.
Updated: Monday, March 2, 2026 at 06:27:03 AM CST
